# Shell completion for leaf commands offers useful values (file paths, existing
# secret paths, user names, flags incl. globals) instead of the bare "help"
# command that urfave/cli's default completer prints. Completion is driven by
# the hidden --generate-shell-completion flag the shell scripts append.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Seal the README created by sesam init so a sealed object exists on disk under
# .sesam/objects for the secret-path completion below.
exec sesam seal

# `sesam add <TAB>`: the positional is a brand-new path, so completion prints
# nothing and lets the shell fall back to its own file completion. The key
# regression guard: it must NOT print the "help" command.
exec sesam add --generate-shell-completion
! stdout .

# `sesam add --g<TAB>`: completes the command's own --group flag.
exec sesam add --g --generate-shell-completion
stdout '--group'

# `sesam add --s<TAB>`: inherited global flags are offered too.
exec sesam add --s --generate-shell-completion
stdout '--sesam-dir'

# `sesam rm <TAB>`: completes existing secret paths globbed from .sesam/objects
# (no decryption needed), not "help".
exec sesam rm --generate-shell-completion
stdout 'README\.md'
! stdout '^help$'

# `sesam rm -<TAB>`: flag completion folds in both command and global flags.
exec sesam rm - --generate-shell-completion
stdout '--identity'

# Add a second user so user-name completion has more than the admin.
exec sesam tell --user bob --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --group dev

# `sesam kill --user <TAB>`: completes known user names from .sesam/signkeys.
exec sesam kill --user --generate-shell-completion
stdout '^admin$'
stdout '^bob$'

# `sesam tell --user <TAB>` likewise completes users instead of printing "help".
exec sesam tell --user --generate-shell-completion
stdout '^bob$'
